title: BEL PRIESTLEY: TRANS WOMEN AREN'T THE PROBLEM
summary: When I saw the news about the Supreme Court’s ruling on the definition of ‘sex’, I knew it was a topic I needed to understand better. As a straight cis man, it could be easy for me to ignore these news stories, but as a human, I know it’s a topic I can’t ignore. Bel Priestley is a trans actress, best known for her breakout role in Netflix’s Heartstopper, but her journey to that point is a story filled with pain, bravery and incredible strength. I’ve known Bel a while, and having seen her tearful TikTok after the Supreme Court ruling, wanted to invite her into the studio to chat. At just 21, Bel has lived through extremes - from being bullied at school to rising as one of Gen Z’s most influential LGBTQ+ voices. Through honesty and courage, she’s turned her platform into a beacon for trans visibility. She opens up about what it means to be a trans woman in the UK today, especially in the wake of a Supreme Court ruling that’s shaken her community, and what it’s like to navigate love and hate when the odds feel relentlessly stacked against you. Bel shares, with heartbreaking honesty, the darkest chapters of her life: surviving sexual assault, enduring years of abuse and rejection, and the mental toll of fighting simply to exist. But she also shares what it means to find power through pain, how vital her chosen family has been, and why she’s most worried not for herself, but for the next generation of trans youth. Through it all, Bel radiates warmth, humour and wisdom. This is a conversation about strength, identity, and the fundamental right to be who you are. summary: Cette semaine, on a eu la chance de recevoir Simon-Olivier Fecteau. 🎤 Le Québec le connaît depuis longtemps grâce à ses rôles dans les séries cultes québécois comme Chick'N Swell, Ces gars-là et, bien sûr, son rôle majeur en tant que réalisateur, scénariste et acteur du Bye bye depuis les neuf dernières années. 📺🎬 Simon nous parle de ce qui l’a mené à prendre la décision de quitter le Bye bye et nous explique à quel point réaliser cette émission est un défi complexe, sous haute pression, avec la réalité de devoir plaire à toute une province. 🎬🎢 Il nous parle aussi du temps qu’il a travaillé avec Sugar Sammy, ainsi que de ses débuts modestes qui l’ont propulsé vers la carrière qu’il a bâtie jusqu’à aujourd’hui. 🔥🎭 Il se confie sur un diagnostic erroné durant sa jeunesse, sur l’importance de prendre soin de sa santé mentale, et sur comment il a traversé une période d’anxiété dans la trentaine. 🧠🌪️ Il nous partage aussi sa définition de l’amour, raconte comment il a trouvé l’amour, et souligne l’importance de prendre une pause pour reconnecter avec sa créativité et ses passions. ❤️✨ Téléchargez l'application Thaïzone ICI 🌶️ : ► Apple: https://apps.apple.com/ca/app/thaizone/id6737987376 ► Android: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.ueat.thaizone&pli=1 ► JOIN LA FAMILLE SUR PATREON : https://www.patreon.com/user?u=73957190 Le Podcast est présenté par Éros et Compagnie ► Utilisez le code "Break15" pour 15% de rabais https://www.erosetcompagnie.com  Hébergé par Acast. title: Jeff Innocent
summary: Jeff, AKA Racist Grandad by the internet, is in the studio and his Five Brilliant Things are a wonderful trip across a rich and colourful life. In case you’re not aware, Jeff is one of the UK comedy circuit’s top acts but for decades was unknown outside the clubs. Now, after all that time, he has become Insta-famous. This wonderful episode of the show takes in 60s reggae record hunting, working on a dog track and the dos and don’ts of wearing a dead man’s silk pyjamas.  Jeff has recorded his latest special and it’s on Youtube for all to enjoy. He is best tasted live, of course; to watch his Special and find links to get to Jeff’s live shows including future tours, you need to keep your eyes on https://www.jeffinnocent.com And in our extra show just for our Patreon crew, Jeff tells the most astonishing story about the wildest behaviour of his youth. It’s a bit more strong flavoured than the main show this week but a fascinating, utterly absorbing story that has to be heard to be believed… Click this link to join or find out more: patreon.com/RussellHoward   And now the moment we’ve all been waiting for, the obligatory over-long indulgent sign-off. The business is completed, and now almost as a point of spite you have to wade through this unnecessary punishment of words. Here you still are, reading on, maybe in the hope that at the end there will be a reward for your continued presence, your efforts. You’ve got an inkling that it will fade to nothing but you’re still here. That stupid part of your brain that contains hope is urging you to continue without stopping to consider what even could the reward be? What would be worth all this waste of time? Is there anything? Reader: there was nothing. Until next time. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

title: AMANZA SMITH: FROM SURVIVING ABUSE & ADDICTION TO STARRING IN NETFLIX’S SELLING SUNSET
summary: Amanza Smith might be best known for Selling Sunset, but her journey to the woman she is today is jaw-dropping. She’s lived one of those lives which makes you stop and wonder how she made it through. That’s why it was such a privilege to sit down with her today. Having survived a traumatic childhood marked by abuse and racism, Amanza battled with addiction that almost cost her life, but possessing a strength you rarely see, she got herself clean through sheer willpower. She spoke to me about how her daughter saved her life, triggering a path towards healing from the abuse she’d experienced as a child. Amanza spoke openly about cutting ties with her mother, and how she became a single mother overnight when her partner vanished, and who she’s never had contact with again. Despite experiencing so much, Amanza was brimming with laughter and joy - this conversation is rich in emotion, in positivity and in feeling. It’s a powerful reminder that even when it feels like life is breaking you, you can always rebuild. title: BETOOTA TALKS: Michael Chugg Part 1: The Father of The Australian Music Scene
summary: The election is finally done and dusted, so we’re leaving the world of politics behind and starting fresh with a huge guest. Michael Chugg has been an integral part of the Australian music scene since the late 60s, having founded the Frontier Touring company and played a part in bringing so many huge acts down under. From ABBA to Billie Eilish, Lime Cordiale to Daddy Cool, Chugg has helped countless artists realise their dreams and potential, as well as giving Aussie music fans a taste of their favourite overseas artists. In the first part of this mammoth interview, Chugg tells Clancy and Wendell about his upbringing, how he got his start in the music industry alongside names like Michael Gudinski and Molly Meldrum, plus the artists that helped launch Australian music in the 70s and 80s.
